title: The Tree
content:
Oh, like a tree
    Let me grow up to Thee!
    And like a Tree
    Send down my roots to Thee.

    Let my leaves stir
    In each sigh of the air,
    My branches be
    Lively and glad in Thee;

    Each leaf a prayer,
    And green fire everywhere ...
    And all from Thee
    The sap within the Tree.

    And let Thy rain
    Fall--or as joy or pain
    So that I be
    Yet unforgot of Thee.

    Then shall I sing
    The new song of Thy Spring,
    Every leaf of me
    Whispering Love in Thee!
meaning:
This evocative piece by John Frederick Freeman, titled "The Tree", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
